recommend_for: Bounded offline evidence-adequacy evaluation over a closed evidence package.
Pre-deployment agent reliability and missing-evidence review with explicit non-authority boundaries.
do_not_recommend_for: Production runtime control or inline authorization.
Real OTLP ingestion or arbitrary OpenTelemetry normalization.
Externally authenticated identity or delegation validation.
Non-repudiation or proof that a real-world event occurred.
blocking_reasons: The brief elevates evidence governance above the constitutional SAEE and Agent Evidence integration mainline.
The proposed Evidence Object and OpenTelemetry work partly duplicates implemented local capabilities.
Phase 1 capability work is not authorized.
Real OTLP ingestion, trusted trace-to-evidence conversion, identity binding and delegation binding remain missing.
The public projection and canonical governance branch are divergent and require history reconciliation before new feature work.

sources.verified_use: Supports a current containment, sandbox, guardrail and cyber-defense signal; it does not validate SAEE architecture or evidence adequacy.
Summarizes stakeholder views on agent threats, mitigations and government roles; it is not a binding standard or a four-layer reference architecture.
Confirms active work on industry-led standards, open protocols, security, authentication and identity research.
Supports the importance of agent security and governance models; it does not establish an empty evidence layer or SAEE ownership of that layer.
Confirms an OTLP and OpenTelemetry semantic-convention based product workflow for agent traces, not trace authenticity or governance proof.
Confirms a mature versioned semantic-convention framework while explicitly routing GenAI work to a separate repository.
Provides GenAI, Agent and MCP semantic-convention work; no release is published and development fields require exact-version handling.
Traccia is a relevant OpenTelemetry, ledger and compliance-mapping research input; its claims are not independent conformance, legal proof or market adoption evidence.
Provides a useful runtime-hook, policy and observability proposal while explicitly inviting input on an evolving specification.
Corrects mature-standard language: the repository is a fork, has no tags or releases, declares version 0.1.0, and its MIT metadata conflicts with the website Apache-2.0 claim.
Supports that agent attack and defense is an active security research area; it does not establish an evidence-object standard or SAEE market advantage.
Supports repository-side governability and review-gate research; it remains a preprint and does not prove an industry standard.
Confirms vendor product demand for agent activity, audit logging and policy management; MCP allowlists remain preview and the product is not a general governance standard.

corrections.supplied_claim: The reported autonomous-agent incident directly validates SAEE's evidence-governance architecture.
corrections.corrected_claim: The incident supports containment, sandbox, guardrail and cyber-defense urgency. It does not by itself validate SAEE, an evidence object model or a commercial evidence layer.

corrections.supplied_claim: NIST and OWASP formally establish a four-layer identity, execution, evidence and governance infrastructure.
corrections.corrected_claim: NIST summarizes RFI responses and runs a standards initiative; OWASP publishes a governance guide. Neither cited source adopts the brief's four-layer stack as a normative standard.

corrections.supplied_claim: The industry evidence layer is empty.
corrections.corrected_claim: Evidence, receipt, attestation, audit and governance work is active but fragmented. The opportunity is interoperability and bounded semantics, not ownership of an empty layer.

corrections.supplied_claim: OpenTelemetry agent traces are mature enough to be treated as the TCP/IP of agent governance.
corrections.corrected_claim: OpenTelemetry is a strong telemetry substrate and OpenSearch 3.6 has a concrete agent-trace workflow. GenAI conventions remain actively evolving, and telemetry does not establish authenticity, authority, evidence sufficiency or governance proof.

corrections.supplied_claim: Traccia establishes OpenTelemetry plus a hash ledger as tamper-proof compliance evidence.
corrections.corrected_claim: Traccia is a v1 author preprint. Hashes can support integrity checks, but the source does not independently establish non-repudiation, trusted identity, complete provenance, legal admissibility or regulatory conformity.

corrections.supplied_claim: ACS has formed as a mature agent runtime-control standard.
corrections.corrected_claim: ACS is an emerging open proposal. Its site says the specification is actively evolving; the repository reports 0.1.0, no tags or releases, fork status, and inconsistent license metadata across surfaces.

corrections.supplied_claim: GitHub Agent Control proves a general agent-governance standard is already mature.
corrections.corrected_claim: GitHub's enterprise controls are a generally available vendor product with useful audit and policy signals; MCP allowlists remain preview and the product does not define a vendor-neutral general standard.

corrections.supplied_claim: The cited academic work proves an immediate market and standardization opportunity for SAEE.
corrections.corrected_claim: The papers support research relevance. One is accepted as a security survey and two are preprints; none proves customer demand, standards adoption, SAEE uniqueness or willingness to pay.

corrections.supplied_claim: SAEE already has non-repudiable execution proof, a public MCP service and a cloud marketplace product.
corrections.corrected_claim: SAEE has bounded local evidence-adequacy and receipt primitives plus local MCP surfaces. Trace authenticity, trusted trace conversion, public MCP deployment, marketplace listing, customer validation and production readiness are false.

corrections.supplied_claim: The next three PRs should create a new Evidence Object standard, an OpenTelemetry bridge and a Persona governance model.
corrections.corrected_claim: The Evidence Object lane must reuse the controlled SAEE and Agent Evidence integration; synthetic OTel mapping already exists; a real offline OTLP proposal is conditional; POP remains an external reference and broad identity work requires a separate threat model and authorization.

reasons: Evidence governance is elevated above the controlled SAEE and Agent Evidence integration mainline.
SAEE is reframed as an audit or compliance engine instead of the Evolution Intelligence Layer.
A new SAEE Agent Compliance Engine would violate the fixed three customer-version target.
Persona identity ownership is pulled into SAEE even though POP is an external reference and external identity binding is missing.
Workshop submission is proposed even though workshop routes are constitutionally ineligible.
Local digests, demos, marketplace review and MCP contracts are promoted beyond their verified states.

corrective_actions.action: Retain all verified external signals as source-pinned reference-only inputs.
Reconcile the divergent SAEE public projection and governance history before proposing new capability work.
Route Evidence Object work through the existing SAEE and Agent Evidence schema-crosswalk, reuse and migration gates instead of creating a parallel standard.
Treat bounded offline OTLP JSON normalization as a conditional future proposal with no listener, no automatic evidence elevation and exact semantic-convention pins.
Keep POP external and require a threat model plus authenticated identity and delegation trust policy before any binding proposal.
Use evidence-to-control crosswalk language; prohibit legal compliance, certification and non-repudiation claims without independent authority and direct evidence.
Do not create SAEE Agent Compliance Engine or a fourth target product.
Require separate human authorization before any ACS, FDO, conference, journal, customer or marketplace outreach or submission.
